title: TAS inputs v2
description: Design specification (the contract the converter must meet) plus operating points (specific test conditions within that contract). Flat — no designRequirements wrapper. All values in SI units (V, A, W, °C, Hz). The fields here are REQUIREMENTS only — design choices like modulationType / controlMode / operatingMode are not inputs, they are decisions made by the design pipeline.

name.description: Optional human-readable design name (e.g. '60W 48V to 5V buck for industrial sensor').

inputVoltage.description: Input voltage range in Volts. The converter must operate across this range. Use {minimum, nominal, maximum} for ranged inputs; {nominal} alone for fixed-input designs.

outputVoltage.description: Output voltage in Volts. Use {nominal} alone for fixed outputs; {minimum, maximum} for adjustable outputs.

outputCurrent.description: Output current range in Amperes. Provide either outputCurrent or outputPower (or both).

efficiencyTarget.description: Target efficiency as a fraction (0.94 = 94%). Optional — agents will try to maximise efficiency regardless, but a target gates the design review.

isolationVoltage.description: Required galvanic isolation in Volts (working voltage per IEC-62368). Null or omitted = non-isolated.

ambientTemperature.description: Operating ambient temperature range in °C. Worst case is the maximum — used for Tj derating calculations.

switchingFrequency.description: Switching frequency constraint in Hz. Three usage modes: (1) {nominal: 350e3} = fixed-frequency design pinned at 350 kHz; (2) {minimum: 50e3, maximum: 250e3} = the design must operate within this range — used for variable-frequency topologies (LLC, COT, hysteretic) or to exclude EMC-sensitive bands (AM 530–1700 kHz, audio <20 kHz, ISM bands); (3) omit entirely = the agent picks freely. The per-operating-point ACTUAL fsw at each test condition is reported in outputs.operatingPoints[].kpis.switchingFrequency, not here. Test conditions are (Vin, Iout/Pout, Tamb) only.

controlMode.description: Required feedback control architecture. Pin this when the choice is forced — by a stock controller IC, by load-step stability requirements (e.g. average current mode for 48 V automotive), or by an existing reference design being cloned. Omit entirely = the control-designer picks. Picked once for the converter; cannot vary per operating point.

modulationType.description: Required modulation scheme. Pin this when transient response, light-load efficiency, or topology constraints force the choice (e.g. Phase-Shift for PSFB; Hysteretic for fastest-transient buck; PWM+PFM hybrid for IC-based light-load efficiency). Omit = agent picks. Picked once for the converter.

operatingMode.description: Required inductor/transformer current operating mode. Pin this when forced by safety/audio requirements (e.g. no DCM for audio amplifiers — audible artifacts), primary-side regulation needs (DCM mandatory for some flyback PSR controllers), or PFC topology choice (CrCM is the boundary mode used by most low-power PFCs). Omit = agent picks. NOTE: this is the *required* mode design-wide. The ACTUAL mode at each operating point is reported in outputs.operatingPoints[].kpis.operatingMode and may differ across OPs (e.g. CCM at full load, DCM at light load is intentional in some designs).

operatingPoints.description: Specific (Vin, Iout/Pout, Tamb) test conditions the design must satisfy. Each entry is one point drawn from the spec ranges above. Outputs.operatingPoints[] entries join to this list by name. Common points: 'full load Vin_min', 'full load Vin_max', 'no load Vin_max', 'startup Vin_min'.

operatingPoint.description: A single test condition. All fields are scalars (not ranges) — this is one specific point in the spec envelope.

name.description: Identifier unique within this inputs document (e.g. 'full load Vin_min'). Used to join with outputs.operatingPoints[].

outputCurrent.description: Iout at this point, A. Provide either outputCurrent or outputPower.
